mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) across rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack triggers earnings recession fears, causing institutional investors to reduce equity allocations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand strength, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, declines in the industrial production index indicate supply-side contraction that outweighs demand resilience, triggering short-term equity sell-offs over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods do not correlate with negative 5-day forward returns (correlation > -0.05), or the signal reverses direction in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index surpasses 235.0, inflation expectations become unanchored, forcing markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 60% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage growth, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or null correlation with 60-day forward returns over rolling 5-year windows, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth decelerates while retail sales accelerate indicates inventory drawdowns and weak underlying demand, prompting equity deleveraging and negative forward returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 qoq annualized < 1.5% and RSAFS mom > 2.0%
failure_condition: The specified GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction coefficient flips sign.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 103.0, capacity utilization approaches peak levels, signaling robust manufacturing output that boosts short-term equity sentiment and generates positive one-week forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of periods where INDPRO exceeds 103.0, or the threshold loses predictive power after adjusting for macro volatility.
